quince-shake-lezzet.name: Quince Shake
instructions: 1. Remove the cores from washing the corners and peeling the shells to the neck.
2. In thin slices, cut into a heat resistant glass rough, place the shellless sides to stay above.
3. Add one tablespoon of sugar on each set of months.
5. 180 degree bake over the oven with aluminum foil and cook until the months soften.
6. Beat milk, eggs, sugar and rice flour.
7. Pour over the pizzles and re oven.
8. Cook for 10 minutes and leave to scratch.

quince-date-chutney.name: Quince-date Chutney

ingredients.prep: peeled, quartered, cored, cut into 1/2-inch dice
peeled, quartered, cored, cut into 1/2-inch dice

instructions: Combine vinegar and brown sugar in heavy large saucepan. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Add quinces; cover and simmer until quinces are tender and liquid is absorbed, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes. Add apples, 1/2 cup water, ginger, coriander, cardamom, salt, cinnamon stick, and chile; bring to simmer over medium heat. Cover and simmer until apples are tender, about 10 minutes. Stir in dates and lime juice. Transfer chutney to bowl and cool. Cover and refrigerate overnight. DO AHEAD: Can be prepared 2 weeks ahead. Keep refrigerated. Bring to room temperature before serving.

quindim-recipe-teleculinaria.name: Quindim Recipe

ingredients.item: To decorate grated coconut, currants and mint leaves

instructions: Drain the grated coconut in the water. In a bowl, mix the sugar with the butter until you get an off-white preparation. Then add the coconut mixture and wrap.

Pass the eggs and the yolks through a fine-network passer and mix everything with the previous preparation. Lie down in a form of pudding (format to taste), previously greased with butter and well sprinkled with sugar.
Place the shape inside a tray with some hot water. Take it in the oven, preheated to 180oC, for about 40 minutes.
Remove the quindim from the oven, pass a knife through the edge of the shape to release it and let it cool for 20 minutes. Deform, decorate with grated coconut, currants and mint leaves and serve.
